title: How to Paint Sylvaneth Soulpods | Grove Guardian | Warhammer Age of Sigmar
description:
Keepers of the soulpod groves, Sylvaneth Grove Guardians grant life to spirits so they may be born to fight for the Everqueen. The bodies of these towering priestesses are formed from a genesis tree, which protects the soulpods – amniotic pods from which Revenants grow. In today’s video, Ollie’s showing you how to paint the unique soulpods of the Grove Guardian model, at every stage of their growth cycle.

Here’s a list of the paints and tools we used in today’s video:

Base:
Screamer Pink

Layer:
Dechala Lilac
Emperor’s Children
Pink Horror
White Scar
Yriel Yellow

Shade:
Carroburg Crimson

Technical:
Lahmian Medium

Brushes:
M Base
S Layer
M Layer

If you enjoyed this and want to learn more, our YouTube channel has an array of beginner-friendly videos to get you started.

title: Painting Death Korps in Steel Legion Colours | Warhammer 40,000
description:
Armageddon, the war world, is the focal setting for Warhammer 40,000's latest animation! And seeing as the Death Korps of Krieg originally started as an alternate colour scheme for the Steel Legion, we thought we'd show you how to paint the colours of the original gas mask wearing Astra Militarum regiment on a Krieg Trooper! Tune in as Ollie shows you everything you need to know about replicating this awesome scheme on your models.

Here’s a list of the paints and tools we used in today’s video:

description:
Imperial Knights are towering war machines – more than a match for vast swathes of enemy infantry, massive monsters, and heavy battle tanks. Bold and ever willing to risk peril, Knights plunge into the fiercest fighting, dealing death with cannons, whirring chainblades, and an arsenal of additional weapons. They’re truly immense and an absolute blast to paint too, despite their massive size! Tune in to this video to see Ollie talk all things painting Imperial Knights!

title: How to Paint Feathers | Warhammer
description:
Feathers appear on a multitude of models throughout the grim dark future, Age of Darkness, and the Mortal Realms. They come in all sorts of shapes, sizes, and colours – from the white, angelic feathers seen on Cherubim to the dark, moody black feathers on Raven Guard and the more exotic tropical feathers displayed on characters and Seraphon. Ollie’s here to show you just a few ways to paint them.

description:

title: Four Ways to Paint Tactical Rocks | Warhammer
description:
Many Warhammer miniatures, especially characters, come adorned with huge stones, rubble, or debris on their bases. The height advantage gained from standing on these so-called tactical rocks is clearly very important to heroes and characters from the 41st millennium, the Age of Darkness, and even the realms of Sigmar. 

Tactical rocks have an important role to play in how our miniatures look. They’re great for world building, allowing for more dynamic and interesting poses, and of course making your favourite characters stand big and tall amongst the rest of your army! 

Tune in to see Ollie show you four simple ways to paint them!

Here’s a list of the paints we used in today’s video:Scheme OneMechanicus Standard Grey – BaseCelestra Grey – BaseAgrax Earthshade – ShadeScheme TwoZandri Dust – BaseSeraphim Sepia – ShadeScreaming Skull – LayerScheme Three Death Guard Green – BaseCoelia Greenshade – ShadeKrieg Khaki – LayerScheme FourAdministratum Grey – LayerCorax White – BaseNuln Oil – ShadeTechnical:Lahmian MediumBrushes:L BaseM ShadeM DryIf you enjoyed this and want to learn more, our YouTube channel has an array of beginner-friendly videos to get you started.

title: Wētā Workshop Neave Blacktalon, The God-King's Assassin, 1:6 Scale Statue | Warhammer Age of Sigmar
description:
Every detail tells the story of Sigmar’s chosen assassin: the splintered war platform beneath her feet, the Orruk helm crushed into the dirt, the storm-forged fury carved into every line of her form.

This dynamic pose was brought to life by our passionate artists, drawing on decades of experience in worldbuilding and character design, distilling Neave’s relentless spirit into collectible form. From the delicate arc of her braided hair in motion, to the painstaking textures of battered iron and bloodied blades, each element has been sculpted, refined, and painted with the same obsessive attention to detail that has come to define our craft.

A hero of the Mortal Realms rendered in full 1:6 scale, Neave Blacktalon, The God-King’s Assassin is a divine example of crafting myth into matter, and fury into form. 

Pre-order now: https://wetanz.com/neave-blacktalon

title: Make your Soulblight Gravelords Vile | Warhammer Age of Sigmar
description:
Love Soulblight Gravelords, but the vampires seem a little bit too clean? Well, Emma has a few simple effects that work a treat to make your blood-sucking noble into a vile, horrific monster, worthy of Nagash’s praise.

Here is the painting guide for the 3 effects: 

Foul Skin:
Base – Ionrach Skin
Shade – Gutrippa Flesh 1:3 Contrast Medium
Highlight – Deepkin Flesh

Dirty Rusty Armour:
Base – Iron Hands Steel
Highlight – Stormhost Silver
Shade – Garaghak’s Sewer 1:2 Contrast Medium
Effect – Troll Slayer Orange 1:2 Lahmian Medium

Old and Fresh Blood:
Old dried blood -–Cygor Brown
Old dried blood —Mephiston Red 1:2 Lahmian Medium
Fresh blood – Blood For the Blood God

Eyes and Teeth:
Eyes and mouth – Abaddon Black
Teeth – Corax White

Brushes used:
S Base
S Dry
S Layer
M Layer

description:

title: How to paint Archaon, Exalted Grand Marshal of the Apocalypse.
description:
This epic Citadel miniature requires an epic painting tutorial that is worthy of the Chaos Lord himself. And who better to show you how to paint it than our very own Duncan Rhodes. In it he shows you a great variety of tips and techniques including great ways to get a fantastic blend on both Dorghar's tail and Archaon's sword, the Slayer of kings as well as ways to paint Dorghar's 3 Chaos heads. So go grab your paints and brushes and let's begin.

Stages:-

00:00 - Intro
01:18 - Dorghar's body
23:27 - Dorghar's wings
32:23 - Dorghars heads
50:55 - Armour and shared details 
1:00:24 - Archaon's unique details
1:15:42 - Final details 
1:22:40 - Base details

title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: How to paint Stormcast Liberators & Prosecutors.
description:
In this tutorial, Duncan shows you how to paint both the Stormcast Liberators and the Stormcast Prosecutors. He shows you a great way to paint their gold amour, the Prosecutors wings and a great way to paint their bases.  On top of all that, he also shows you how to apply their transfers.

Stages:
0:00 Intro
1:15 Armour
8:23 Scrolls 
11:40 Armour highlights 
15:44 Red Crest 
17:02 Prosecutor Wings 
19:45 Base 
22:08 Transfers

title: Warhammer 40,000: How to paint Blood Angels
description:

As usual, all the methods and techniques shown can be used on all types of Adpetus Astartes miniatures and chapters.
